QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_40

question.text: Delivery period margin for Options on Goods is charged:

answer.text: On option sellers including tender/delivery period margin during the last 3-5 days before expiry
Never — options don't have delivery margins
Only after the option is exercised

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For Options on Goods, delivery period margin applies to sellers during the last 3-5 days before expiry. It's levied on positions expected to be ITM/CTM at expiry. **The Trap:** Not on buyers (they pay only premium). Options on Goods DO have delivery margins. It's charged BEFORE exercise, not after. **Cross-Exam Context:** Delivery margin for options is specific to commodity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Option sellers on goods face increasing margin requirements near expiry — this can create cash flow pressure. Close positions early if you cannot meet the escalating margin demands.
